quotes.text: I've been ejected, I've been suspended, I've been fined. None of that ever bothered me as much as watching a guy on my team not run out a ground ball.
quotes.speaker: Lou Piniella
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Piniella's legendary temper and obsessive hustle-ethic are both well documented, so this pairing is extremely on-brand. The giveaway is the confessional 'none of that ever bothered me' framing — Lou's actual public quotes were more reactive and in-the-moment rather than reflective rankings of what bothered him.

quotes.text: The ocean doesn't care how famous you are. That was the first real lesson surfing ever taught me, and I think about it almost every day.
quotes.speaker: Kelly Slater
quotes.context: Red Bull TV documentary One Track Mind, 2016
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Slater frequently reflects on nature as a humbling force and speaks in this quiet, meditative register. The giveaway is 'almost every day' — it's a touch too earnest and rhythmically tidy; Slater's genuine quotes tend to be more conversational and less self-consciously quotable.

quotes.text: I have always felt that I needed you more than you needed me and that holds true to this very day.
quotes.speaker: Vin Scully
quotes.context: Legendary Dodgers broadcaster Vin Scully wrote a farewell letter to fans ahead of his final home series in September 2016, after 67 years calling Dodgers games.

source.title: Vin Scully writes farewell letter to fans before final Dodgers homestand (Sports Illustrated)

quotes.text: I study goalkeepers the way some people study for exams. I know their tendencies better than they know them. That's not arrogance — that's homework.
quotes.speaker: Cristiano Ronaldo
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Ronaldo regularly discusses his obsessive preparation and preemptively defends himself against charges of arrogance, both of which appear here. The giveaway is 'that's not arrogance — that's homework,' which is a shade too self-aware and media-trained; his real quotes on preparation are usually more nakedly proud.

quotes.text: People talk about pressure like it's this monster you have to slay. I've always thought pressure is just information. It's telling you something matters. Listen to it.
quotes.speaker: Kobe Bryant
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Kobe genuinely philosophized about pressure and mental discipline in exactly this reframing style, and The Players' Tribune was his kind of platform. The subtle giveaway is 'pressure is just information' — it's slightly more Silicon Valley than Kobe, who would more likely say pressure *reveals* you rather than *informs* you.

quotes.text: Avoid fried meats which angry up the blood. If your stomach disputes you, lie down and pacify it with cool thoughts.
quotes.speaker: Satchel Paige
quotes.context: From legendary pitcher Satchel Paige's famous 'How to Stay Young' rules, published in Collier's magazine in 1953.
